blurb: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, the government department leading AI policy, investment and digital regulation.

publications.title: Artificial Intelligence Playbook for the UK Government
publications.summary: DSIT-led guidance on using AI safely, effectively and securely across government. Sets out 10 principles for civil servants and explains how to select, buy and deploy AI; expands and retitles the January 2024 Generative AI Framework for HMG.

publications.title: AI Cyber Security Code of Practice
publications.summary: DSIT (in collaboration with NCSC) voluntary Code of Practice setting baseline cyber security principles for AI systems, with an implementation guide. Updated after the summer 2024 call for views; to be submitted to ETSI as the basis for global standard TS 104 223.

news.title: UK backs new AI labs to make technology cheaper, more reliable and easier to use
news.summary: DSIT press notice announcing government backing for new AI research labs aimed at making the technology cheaper and more reliable.

news.title: AI tool to slash planning decision times as government accelerates push to build 1.5 million homes
news.summary: DSIT announces an AI planning tool to speed up planning decisions in support of the housebuilding target.

news.title: Britain powers ahead on AI with billions of pounds of new investment and thousands of jobs secured as London Tech Week wraps up
news.summary: DSIT summarises AI investment and jobs announcements made during London Tech Week 2026.

notes: News sourced from DSIT's official gov.uk news atom feed (news-and-communications.atom filtered to DSIT). publications[] backfilled 2026-06-28 with two first-party DSIT AI policy publications verified directly on gov.uk publication pages: AI Playbook (pub 2025-02-10) and AI Cyber Security Code of Practice (pub 2025-01-31, with NCSC). These are department-authored policy outputs, distinct from the dated press notices in news[]. No filings/controversies where DSIT is a named legal party found in this window. schema_cleanup 2026-06-28: added feed-sync-safe publication type values to DSIT policy publications.
